data.title: Trump floated the idea of 15% govt stake in Norfolk Southern/Union Pacific - report
data.description:
President Donald Trump recently expressed interest in the federal government taking a 15% stake in the proposed combination of Union Pacific (UNP) and Norfolk Southern (NSC).

Trump told Fortune Magazine that he wanted the government to have a stake in the combined railroad.

“I got another one coming, a railroad,” Trump told Fortune of a deal that was currently in the works. “They want to expand. They want to merge, a enormous railroad; they want to merge. And I say, ‘Well, I want 15% of the railroad if you’re going to merge.'”

The companies declined Trump's request, the president told Fortune, though he said he thinks they will reconsider.

The Forbes article comes after the nation's railroad regulator on Thursday said it accepted the application from the companies about their proposed deal, though it has paused its review as the authority needs more information from the companies.

The companies said in a statement  on Thursday that they now expect the deal to close in mid-2027 after previously forecasting a close in early 2027 following the delay from the regulator.

The White House and the STB didn't to requests for comment from Fortune, nor did Union Pacific or Norfolk Southern.

data.title: Catalyst Watch: FedEx splits, tech CEOs hit Computex, Quantinuum IPO, and the jobs report
data.description:
Welcome to Seeking Alpha's Catalyst Watch - a breakdown of some of next week's actionable events that stand out. Read more about the events that may impact stock prices next week.

 

Monday - June 1

  • Volatility watch - Netflix (NFLX) and Globalstar (GSAT) have seen options volatility increase over the last week. The most overbought stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include Momentus (MNTS), Hyliion (HYLN), and Redwire (RDW). The most oversold stocks per their 14-day Relative Strength Index include Verra Mobility (VRRM) and Suja Life (SUJA). Short interest is elevated on Kohl's (KSS) after its +20% earnings move.
  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE).
  • Dividend watch - Companies that have an ex-dividend date coming next week include McDonald's (MCD), Halliburton (HAL), Cigna (CI), and Western Digital (WDC).
  • IPO watch - Quantinuum (QNT) headlines a busy week for IPOs. The Honeywell (HON)-backed quantum computing platform is looking to raise up to $1.05B and to value it at roughly $12.7B. Other IPOs expected to price and begin trading include aerospace components manufacturer Applied Aerospace & Defense (AADX), silver miner Sunshine Silver Mining & Refining (GATO), Quantinuum (QNT), coastal property insurer Safepoint Holdings (SFPT), and gas energy firm Innio Holding (INIO). The analyst quiet period ends on Hawkeye 360 (HAWK), Suja Life (SUJA), Rare Earths Americas (REA), and Odyssey Therapeutics (ODTX) to free up analysts to post ratings.
  • Data watch - Key data reports due to arrive during the first few days of the week include updates on monthly deliveries from Nio (NIO), XPeng (XPEV), Li Auto (LI), and BYD Company (BYDDF). Other reports of high interest to investors will be the monthly Macau gross gaming revenue release, the monthly update on Class 8 truck orders, firearm background check data from the FBI, and discount broker DARTs reports.
  • All day - FedEx (FDX) will officially split off its less-than-truckload trucking arm, FedEx Freight (FDXF), into a separate, publicly traded company while keeping the express and ground parcel businesses inside FedEx Corp. The move is structured as a tax-efficient spin-off. FedEx Freight will be a component of the S&P 500 Index right out of the gate.
  • All day - The four-day Computex Taipei event will begin. The AI-first show includes a wall-to-wall lineup of chip CEOs, heavyweight forums on physical AI, and a dense calendar of launches spanning PCs, data centers, edge, and robotics. Qualcomm (QCOM) CEO Cristiano Amon, Intel (INTC) CEO Lip-Bu Tan, Arm (ARM) CEO Rene Haas, and Marvell Technologies (MRVL) CEO Matt Murphy will all give keynotes or major presentations. Nvidia is effectively co-branding its Computex presence as "NVIDIA GTC Taipei at COMPUTEX," running across the week of the show and CEO Jensen Huang delivers a high-profile keynote. Cisco (CSCO) also plans a big presence.
  • All day - Snowflake Summit 26 next week will begin in San Francisco. Snowflake's (SNOW) four-day flagship conference in San Francisco will be focused on "Making AI Real for Business," with heavy emphasis on enterprise data, generative/agentic AI, and applications.
  • All day - The Atlantic hurricane season officially begins. Traders expect more volatility than normal around crude oil futures this year if any major hurricanes develop near offshore drilling assets.

 

Tuesday - June 2

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Palo Alto Networks (PANW), Dollar General (DG), Ulta Beauty (ULTA), Signet Jewelers (SIG), and Yesway (YSWY), which will be reporting for the first time as a public company. Options trading implies double-digit swings for Oddity Tech (ODD) and Victoria's Secret (VSCO) after their reports are released.
  • All day - Federal Reserve Bank of Minneapolis President Neel Kashkari is scheduled to participate in a panel discussion at the 2026 Bank of Korea International Conference.
  • All day - The Microsoft Build event will begin in San Francisco. The flagship developer showcase will kick off with a keynote from Microsoft (MSFT) CEO Satya Nadella.
  • All day - The two-day Deutsche Bank Basic Materials Conference will begin. The conference will include a long list of presentations from senior executives across chemicals, mining, industrials, and related basic materials companies. 
  • 8:30 a.m. Cleveland Federal Reserve Bank President Beth Hammack will give remarks and participate in a conversation on monetary policy.

 

Wednesday - June 3

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Broadcom (AVGO), Crowdstrike (CRWD), Five Below (FIVE), Medtronic (MDT), and Macy's (M). Options trading implies double-digit swings for Netskope (NTSK) and Petco Health and Wellness (WOOF) after their reports are released.
  • All day - Honeywell (HON) will hold an Aerospace Investor Day event ahead of the planned spinoff of Honeywell Aerospace later in the year.
  • 3:00 p.m. The Federal Reserve will release its Beige Book Report. The report will summarize current economic conditions across the Fed's districts.
  • Postmarket - Costco (COST) will release its May sales report.

 

Thursday - June 4

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Ciena (CIEN), Samsara (IOT), Rubrik (RBRK), Docusign (DOCU), and Lululemon Athletica (LULU). Options trading implies a double-digit swing for X-Energy (XE) and Planet Labs (PL) after they report.
  • All day - Notable investor events include Radian Group's (RDN) Investor Day, Solstice Advanced Materials' (SOLS) business update call, and Lucid Group's (LCID) annual meeting.
  • All day - Boralex (BRLXF) (BLX:CA) shareholders will vote on the acquisition offer from Brookfield Asset Management (BAM) (BEP) (BN) and Caisse de Depot et Placement du Quebec.

 

Friday - June 5

  • All day - The Manheim Used Vehicle Value Index will be released.
  • All day - It is the one-year anniversary date of the Circle (CRCL) IPO.
  • All day - The live-action Masters of the Universe movie from Amazon MGM Studios and Mattel Studios is set to hit U.S. theaters. Mattel (MAT) has a significant merchandising program tied to the film.
  • All day - The Summer Game Fest conference will take place. The video game conference could generate buzz that is relevant to Sony's (SONY) PlayStation, Nintendo (NTDOY), Microsoft's (MSFT) Xbox, and major third-party publishers like Capcom, Square Enix, Bandai Namco, CD Projekt, and Epic Games.
  • 8:30 a.m. The May jobs report will be released. Economists expect 93K job additions for the month and for the unemployment rate to remain at 4.3%.

 

Seeking Alpha Editor Josh Fineman contributed to this article.

data.title: Blue Owl’s Lipschultz says private credit 'freak out' has passed
data.description:
Blue Owl Capital (OWL) co-CEO Marc Lipschultz on Friday signaled a shift in investor sentiment toward private credit (VPC) (BIZD) (PRIV), suggesting the recent period of heightened concern over the asset class may be subsiding.

“It’s not the same freak-out conversation,” Lipschultz said at a Bernstein conference, noting that investor tone has shifted from panic to renewed interest in direct lending products. He acknowledged that elevated redemptions are likely to continue for the rest of the year, with moderated inflows expected, but emphasized that “the super cycle is already behind us.”

Lipschultz highlighted the resilience of Blue Owl’s (OWL) redemption structure, noting that the 5% caps on redemptions “work really well.” The firm took in $3B in loan repayments while only $1B went out the door for redemptions, which he described as a “net cash generating system.”

On credit quality, Lipschultz said it remains “really high” while acknowledging emerging problems in unexpected areas. “Software companies (IGV) that three years ago, they never would have thought would be on that list” are now showing stress, he noted.

Lipschultz said he remains bullish on the RIA channel, attributing redemption activity to roughly 1% of investors driving the outflows.

data.title: Bristol-Myers multiple myeloma data boosts C4
data.description:
C4 Therapeutics (CCCC) reached a 52-week high on Friday after a treatment developed by Bristol-Myers Squibb (BMY), similar to the company’s lead candidate, generated impressive survival data in a late-stage trial for multiple myeloma, a blood cancer.

The upsurge came after BMS (BMY) announced that its multiple myeloma candidate mezigdomide, which is designed to degrade Ikaros protein among other functions, improved cancer-free survival by more than 50% in its Phase 3 SUCCESSOR-2 trial.

Ikaros is encoded by the IKZF1 transcription factor, which is responsible for multiple myeloma biology and targeted by C4’s (CCCC) lead asset cemsidomide. “These are proteins that play a pretty big role in how multiple myeloma works,” Seeking Alpha analyst Petri Dish Reports said of IKZF1 and its sister transcription factor IKZF3 in a recent bullish thesis on the company.

C4 (CCCC) plans to share data from a Phase 1b trial evaluating cemsidomide in combination with approved MM therapies in H2 2026.

According to Bloomberg, Jefferies analyst Akash Tewari, in a note to clients on April 30, expected positive readthrough to C4 (CCCC) from Bristol-Myers’ (BMY) data, citing a combination-focused dosing strategy and other factors. Tewari has a Buy rating on the stock.

data.title: JPMorgan's Jamie Dimon says market exuberance “not bad,” but warns of risks
data.description:
JPMorgan Chase (JPM) CEO Jamie Dimon on Friday characterized current equity markets (SPY) (DIA) (QQQ) as “exuberant,” but struck a notably measured tone about the rally’s sustainability.

Speaking with CNBC during an interview at the Reagan National Economic Forum in California, Dimon acknowledged the strong market performance, including Micron’s (MU) rapid ascent to a trillion-dollar market cap amid the AI (AIQ) infrastructure boom.

“I view the market as exuberant,” Dimon said. “I’ve seen this before. Exuberance can go on a long time, and it’s not bad.”

The banking chief pointed to solid fundamentals supporting current valuations, noting that earnings are up 20% this year and many companies have substantial order books. However, he cautioned that “there is also hype in some of the stuff” and flagged tight credit spreads as a potential concern.

“It makes you feel good, but if something goes wrong, those asset prices can come down,” Dimon warned. “Interest rates (US10Y) (US2Y) are gravity to asset prices.”

Despite the cautionary notes, Dimon expressed confidence in JPMorgan’s (JPM) resilience, stating the U.S.'s largest bank can handle rates ranging from 2% to 8%. “We’re not betting our company on either one,” he said.

data.title: ECARX and TPK Holdings to jointly develop LiDAR technology
data.description:
Automotive technology company ECARX Holdings (ECX) and TPK Holding will co-develop LiDAR technology to be used for advanced driver assistance systems ((ADAS)) and AI-enabled autonomous driving systems.

This marks ECARX’s (ECX) entry into the LiDAR sector and leverages the company’s expertise in vehicle computing platforms and system-on-chips ((SoCs)).

Under the  memorandum of business cooperation signed by the two, ECARX (ECX) and TPK Holding will jointly develop the ORCA LiDAR platform, with mass production slated for 2028 at TPK’s Thailand production facility. 

ECARX (ECX) will capitalize on its established relationship with vehicle OEMs and robotaxi operators to develop system integration and sensor fusion, as well as global commercialization, while TPK will focus on optical design, engineering, and high-volume manufacturing of precision components.

London-based ECARX (ECX) shares are rallying Friday, heading into the close with a gain of more than 12%.

data.title: Robinhood approached by states, public-sector organizations for similar versions to 'Trump accounts' - report
data.description:
Several U.S. states and public-sector organizations have approached Robinhood Markets (HOOD) for partnership over their own versions of "Trump accounts", Bloomberg News reported on Friday, citing CFO Shiv Verma.

On Thursday, the Trump administration rolled out its Trump Accounts app, ahead of the program's official launch on July 4. The app enables Americans to open retirement accounts, similar to a 401(k) for children.

The U.S. Treasury chose BNY (BNY), formerly called Bank of New York Mellon, as the financial agent to implement the program. BNY partnered with Robinhood (HOOD) to serve as the brokerage and initial trustee for the accounts.

The initiative could serve as a model for many institutions and organizations that want to have their own similar programs, the CFO reportedly said.

Robinhood stands to gain some potential partners as several states have already rolled out wealth-building programs for children lately, the Bloomberg report noted.
